info.title: Frontend not accessible on port 3001 after run-local.sh

diffs.before:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SERVER_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/server"
WEB_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/web"

SERVER_PORT=8080
WEB_PORT=3001

SERVER_LOG="/tmp/entire-server.log"
WEB_LOG="/tmp/entire-web.log"

if ! command -v lsof >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: lsof is required but not found.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Homebrew is required for managing local MySQL service.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -s "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh" ]; then
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh"
  fi
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Java is not available in PATH.\n'
  printf 'Install Java 25 and try again.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env" ] && [ -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" ]; then
  cp "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" "$SERVER_DIR/.env"
  printf 'Created server/.env from template. Update DB credentials if needed.\n'
fi

printf 'Starting MySQL service (Homebrew)...\n'
brew services start mysql >/dev/null

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Backend already running on port %s.\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
else
  printf 'Starting backend on port %s...\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
  (
    cd "$SERVER_DIR"
    nohup ./gradlew bootRun >"$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Frontend already running on port %s.\n' "$WEB_PORT"
else
  printf 'Starting frontend on port %s...\n' "$WEB_PORT"
  (
    cd "$WEB_DIR"
    nohup pnpm dev >"$WEB_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

printf 'Waiting for services to bind ports...\n'
sleep 4

printf '\nLocal services status:\n'
if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SERVER_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Backend : http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$SERVER_PORT" "$SERVER_PID"
else
  printf '  Backend : not running (check %s)\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  WEB_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Frontend: http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$WEB_PORT" "$WEB_PID"
else
  printf '  Frontend: not running (check %s)\n' "$WEB_LOG"
fi

printf '\nLogs:\n'
printf '  %s\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
printf '  %s\n' "$WEB_LOG"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SERVER_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/server"
WEB_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/web"

SERVER_PORT=8080
WEB_PORT=3001

SERVER_LOG="/tmp/entire-server.log"
WEB_LOG="/tmp/entire-web.log"

if ! command -v lsof >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: lsof is required but not found.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Homebrew is required for managing local MySQL service.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -s "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh" ]; then
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh"
  fi
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! java -version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Java runtime is not available.\n'
  printf 'Install Java 25 and try again.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env" ] && [ -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" ]; then
  cp "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" "$SERVER_DIR/.env"
  printf 'Created server/.env from template. Update DB credentials if needed.\n'
fi

printf 'Starting MySQL service (Homebrew)...\n'
brew services start mysql >/dev/null

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Backend already running on port %s.\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
else
  printf 'Starting backend on port %s...\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
  (
    cd "$SERVER_DIR"
    nohup ./gradlew bootRun >"$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  WEB_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  WEB_CMD="$(ps -p "$WEB_PID" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
  printf 'Frontend already running on port %s (PID %s).\n' "$WEB_PORT" "$WEB_PID"
  if [ -n "$WEB_CMD" ]; then
    printf 'Existing process: %s\n' "$WEB_CMD"
  fi
else
  printf 'Starting frontend on port %s...\n' "$WEB_PORT"
  (
    cd "$WEB_DIR"
    nohup pnpm dev --host 0.0.0.0 --port "$WEB_PORT" >"$WEB_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

printf 'Waiting for services to bind ports...\n'
sleep 4

printf '\nLocal services status:\n'
if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SERVER_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Backend : http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$SERVER_PORT" "$SERVER_PID"
else
  printf '  Backend : not running (check %s)\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  WEB_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Frontend: http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$WEB_PORT" "$WEB_PID"
else
  printf '  Frontend: not running (check %s)\n' "$WEB_LOG"
fi

printf '\nLogs:\n'
printf '  %s\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
printf '  %s\n' "$WEB_LOG"

metadata.preview:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SERVER_PORT=8080
WEB_PORT=3001

stop_by_port() {
  local port="$1"
  local name="$2"
  local pids

  pids="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$port" -sTCP:LISTEN || true)"
  if [ -n "$pids" ]; then
    printf 'Stopping %s on port %s (PID: %s)\n' "$name" "$port" "$pids"
    kill $pids
  else
    printf '%s not running on port %s.\n' "$name" "$port"
  fi
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SERVER_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/server"
WEB_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/web"

SERVER_PORT=8080
WEB_PORT=3001

SERVER_LOG="/tmp/entire-server.log"
WEB_LOG="/tmp/entire-web.log"

if ! command -v lsof >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: lsof is required but not found.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Homebrew is required for managing local MySQL service.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -s "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh" ]; then
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh"
  fi
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Java is not available in PATH.\n'
  printf 'Install Java 25 and try again.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env" ] && [ -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" ]; then
  cp "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" "$SERVER_DIR/.env"
  printf 'Created server/.env from template. Update DB credentials if needed.\n'
fi

printf 'Starting MySQL service (Homebrew)...\n'
brew services start mysql >/dev/null

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Backend already running on port %s.\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
else
  printf 'Starting backend on port %s...\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
  (
    cd "$SERVER_DIR"
    nohup ./gradlew bootRun >"$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Frontend already running on port %s.\n' "$WEB_PORT"
else
  printf 'Starting frontend on port %s...\n' "$WEB_PORT"
  (
    cd "$WEB_DIR"
    nohup pnpm dev >"$WEB_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

printf 'Waiting for services to bind ports...\n'
sleep 4

printf '\nLocal services status:\n'
if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SERVER_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Backend : http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$SERVER_PORT" "$SERVER_PID"
else
  printf '  Backend : not running (check %s)\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  WEB_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Frontend: http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$WEB_PORT" "$WEB_PID"
else
  printf '  Frontend: not running (check %s)\n' "$WEB_LOG"
fi

printf '\nLogs:\n'
printf '  %s\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
printf '  %s\n' "$WEB_LOG"

parts.text:
I checked this end-to-end with your scripts.

- `run-local.sh` was starting the frontend fine on `3001` in my run, but the backend was silently failing (`/tmp/entire-server.log` showed: no Java runtime).
- Root cause: on macOS, `command -v java` can succeed even when only the Apple stub exists (no real JRE/JDK installed), so your script didn’t fail early.
- I updated `run-local.sh` to validate Java properly (`java -version`), so it now fails fast with a clear message instead of continuing in a broken state.
- I also made frontend startup more explicit in `run-local.sh`: it now starts with `--host 0.0.0.0 --port 3001` and prints the existing process command if port `3001` is already occupied.

What changed:
- `run-local.sh`

What you’ll see now:
- If Java is missing, `./run-local.sh` exits with:
  - `Error: Java runtime is not available.`
  - `Install Java 25 and try again.`

Next steps:
1. Install Java 25 (or make sure `java -version` works in your shell).
2. Run `./run-local.sh` again.
3. If `3001` is still blocked, the script now shows which process is already holding that port so you can stop it (`./stop-local.sh` or kill that PID).

  printf 'Error: Homebrew is required for managing local MySQL service.\n'

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SERVER_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/server"
WEB_DIR="$ROOT_DIR/web"

SERVER_PORT=8080
WEB_PORT=3001

SERVER_LOG="/tmp/entire-server.log"
WEB_LOG="/tmp/entire-web.log"

if ! command -v lsof >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: lsof is required but not found.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Homebrew is required for managing local MySQL service.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  if [ -s "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh" ]; then
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$HOME/.sdkman/bin/sdkman-init.sh"
  fi
fi

if ! command -v java >/dev/null 2>&1 || ! java -version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Error: Java runtime is not available.\n'
  printf 'Install Java 25 and try again.\n'
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env" ] && [ -f "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" ]; then
  cp "$SERVER_DIR/.env.dist" "$SERVER_DIR/.env"
  printf 'Created server/.env from template. Update DB credentials if needed.\n'
fi

printf 'Starting MySQL service (Homebrew)...\n'
brew services start mysql >/dev/null

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  printf 'Backend already running on port %s.\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
else
  printf 'Starting backend on port %s...\n' "$SERVER_PORT"
  (
    cd "$SERVER_DIR"
    nohup ./gradlew bootRun >"$SERVER_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  WEB_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  WEB_CMD="$(ps -p "$WEB_PID" -o command= 2>/dev/null || true)"
  printf 'Frontend already running on port %s (PID %s).\n' "$WEB_PORT" "$WEB_PID"
  if [ -n "$WEB_CMD" ]; then
    printf 'Existing process: %s\n' "$WEB_CMD"
  fi
else
  printf 'Starting frontend on port %s...\n' "$WEB_PORT"
  (
    cd "$WEB_DIR"
    nohup pnpm dev --host 0.0.0.0 --port "$WEB_PORT" >"$WEB_LOG" 2>&1 &
  )
fi

printf 'Waiting for services to bind ports...\n'
sleep 4

printf '\nLocal services status:\n'
if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SERVER_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$SERVER_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Backend : http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$SERVER_PORT" "$SERVER_PID"
else
  printf '  Backend : not running (check %s)\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
fi

if lsof -nP -iTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  WEB_PID="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$WEB_PORT" -sTCP:LISTEN | head -n 1)"
  printf '  Frontend: http://127.0.0.1:%s (PID %s)\n' "$WEB_PORT" "$WEB_PID"
else
  printf '  Frontend: not running (check %s)\n' "$WEB_LOG"
fi

printf '\nLogs:\n'
printf '  %s\n' "$SERVER_LOG"
printf '  %s\n' "$WEB_LOG"

parts.text:
Yep, that makes sense — I verified you do have Homebrew JDK installed, but your script was still picking up macOS’s stub Java.

- `brew --prefix openjdk@25` resolves correctly, but `command -v java` was returning `/usr/bin/java` (Apple launcher), which fails at runtime.
- So `run-local.sh` thought Java existed, then backend startup broke, which made local startup look flaky.
- I updated `run-local.sh` to handle this reliably:
  - Added a real Java readiness check (`java -version`) at `run-local.sh:24`.
  - If Java isn’t usable, it now auto-wires Homebrew Java from `openjdk@25` (or `openjdk`) by exporting `JAVA_HOME`/`PATH` at `run-local.sh:37`.
  - Fixed SDKMAN init under `set -u` (it was crashing with `SDKMAN_CANDIDATES_API: unbound variable`) at `run-local.sh:31`.
  - Frontend still starts on 3001, now explicitly with host/port flags at `run-local.sh:80`.

I re-ran and confirmed frontend responds on `http://127.0.0.1:3001` (`HTTP 200`).

If you want, I can also make the backend status wait a little longer before printing “not running” (right now it checks after 4s, but Spring often needs more).
